input.prompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

<current_datetime>2026-05-10T01:02:09.165Z</current_datetime>

I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,

input.initialPrompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

data.content:
Here's a practical approach:

## 1. Define theme resource dictionaries

Create one `ResourceDictionary` per theme (e.g., in a `Themes/` folder):

```xml
<!-- Themes/LightTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
                    x:Class="MyApp.Themes.LightTheme">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#1A1A1A</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#0078D4</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

```xml
<!-- Themes/DarkTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary ...  x:Class="MyApp.Themes.DarkTheme">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#1E1E1E</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#E0E0E0</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#4CC2FF</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

```xml
<!-- Themes/HighContrastTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary ...  x:Class="MyApp.Themes.HighContrastTheme">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#000000</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#FFFF00</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

## 2. Load a default theme in `App.xaml`

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <ResourceDictionary>
        <ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
            <themes:LightTheme />
        </ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
    </ResourceDictionary>
</Application.Resources>
```

## 3. Create a theme-switching service

```csharp
public enum AppTheme { Light, Dark, HighContrast }

public static class ThemeService
{
    public static void ApplyTheme(AppTheme theme)
    {
        var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;

        // Remove the current theme dictionary (always the first one)
        mergedDicts.Clear();

        mergedDicts.Add(theme switch
        {
            AppTheme.Dark         => new Themes.DarkTheme(),
            AppTheme.HighContrast => new Themes.HighContrastTheme(),
            _                     => new Themes.LightTheme(),
        });

        // Persist the choice
        Preferences.Set("app_theme", theme.ToString());
    }

    public static void LoadSavedTheme()
    {
        if (Enum.TryParse<AppTheme>(Preferences.Get("app_theme", "Light"), out var saved))
            ApplyTheme(saved);
    }
}
```

Call `ThemeService.LoadSavedTheme()` in your `App` constructor (after `InitializeComponent`).

## 4. Use `DynamicResource` in your views

This is the key — `DynamicResource` re-evaluates when the dictionary changes; `StaticResource` does not.

```xml
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource PageBackground}">
    <Label Text="Hello" TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" />
    <Button BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource Accent}" Text="Go" />
</ContentPage>
```

## 5. Settings page picker

```xml
<Picker x:Name="ThemePicker" Title="Theme"
        ItemsSource="{Binding Themes}"
        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedTheme}"
        SelectedIndexChanged="OnThemeChanged" />
```

```csharp
void OnThemeChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    if (ThemePicker.SelectedItem is string name
        && Enum.TryParse<AppTheme>(name, out var theme))
    {
        ThemeService.ApplyTheme(theme);
    }
}
```

**Key takeaways:**
- One `ResourceDictionary` per theme, all sharing the same keys.
- Swap dictionaries at runtime via `MergedDictionaries.Clear()` + `Add()`.
- Always bind with `DynamicResource` so the UI updates immediately.
- Persist the choice with `Preferences` and restore it on startup.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to set up themed resource dictionaries in a .NET MAUI app with light, dark, and high-contrast themes, plus a settings page for switching between them. This is a general "how-to" question, not a codebase task. I'll provide a concise, practical answer.
